title: Subcommittee Helps Direct How & Where $10-$20 Billion In Climate Change, Wildfire Prevention, & Environmental Protection Dollars Will Be Spent

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Senate Budget Committee's Subcommittee on Resources, Environmental Protection and Energy regularly approves more than $12 billion in funding each year.",
    "Recent climate packages have increased the budget approval to over $20 billion.",
    "The California Environmental Protection Agency includes the Air Resources Board and the State Water Resources Control Board, which are pivotal in combating climate change.",
    "Senator Becker has introduced SB 48 to set performance standards aimed at making buildings more energy efficient.",
    "Senator Becker has introduced SB 49 to create incentives for building solar canopies in parking lots across the state.",
    "Senate Bill 1203, known as 'California Zero', mandates the development of a plan to achieve net zero greenhouse gas emissions from buildings, vehicle fleets, and electricity usage by 2035.",
    "Senate Bill 1158 requires power companies like PG&E to disclose the use of clean power in the electricity grid.",
    "Senate Bill 887 mandates that California's energy agencies provide 15-year projections of energy resources and demands."
  ]
}
```
